body:
## Environment
- **Version**: 0.195.0 (wasm), web demo — https://link-assistant.github.io/formal-ai/
- **Mode**: demo off (manual chat), Locale en-US / Europe/Moscow

## Summary
Time-of-day **duration** arithmetic is not routed to the `calculator` tool, so it returns `intent: unknown`. The `calculator` tool description advertises **datetime** support, and plain math (`2 + 2`) and percentages (`8% of $50`) work — but anything involving `HH:MM` time math falls through to "I didn't understand". For a user this is the kind of trivial question a real AI answers instantly, so it reads as "not AI".

## Reproduction (both fail with `intent: unknown`)
1. Natural language: `If a train leaves at 14:00 and arrives at 17:30, how long is the trip?`
2. Direct expression: `17:30 - 14:00`

**Expected:** `3:30` / `3 h 30 min` (via the datetime calculator).
**Actual:** "I didn't understand you. I cannot answer that from local links rules yet…" (intent: unknown).

## Evidence (from the reasoning trace)
For `17:30 - 14:00` the formalization is `(@USER OP:express ?17 30 14 00)` and the invoked tools are `wikipedia_article_question, fact_query, project_lookup, http_fetch, url_navigate, docs_method_explanation, procedural_how_to, web_search, wikipedia_lookup` — **`calculator` is never invoked**, and `tool_calls` shows only `wikipedia_lookup → no_match`. So the gap is in intent routing: `HH:MM` arithmetic / "how long between A and B" is not classified as a calculator/datetime intent.

## Suggested fix
Route `HH:MM` arithmetic and "how long / duration between <time> and <time>" phrasings to the existing datetime path of `calculator` (the same way `2 + 2` and `8% of $50` already reach it). The capability exists; only the classifier/router needs the time-duration pattern.

## Note
Tested while integrating formal-ai as a deterministic verification layer in another project — the deterministic calculator is exactly the strength we want to rely on, which is why the missing datetime routing stood out. Great work overall; reproducibility and the inline reasoning trace are excellent.

comments.body: We also need to make sure our link calculator dependency fully supports all such and similar cases, if not - we should report issue there.

title: Time-of-day duration not routed to calculator: "17:30 - 14:00" and "how long is the trip" return unknown
